query: What is the current level of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as of June 2026, per EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1)? What has been the weekly trajectory over the past 3-6 months (fills or releases)?
answer:
Research complete. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Current SPR Level (EIA series WCSSTUS1):** 325,655 thousand barrels (~325.7 million barrels) as of the week ending June 26, 2026 — the **lowest level since May 1983**, down ~19.1% year-over-year.

**Trajectory over the past 3–6 months (two clear phases):**
- **Dec 2025 – mid-March 2026:** Modest fills, then flat, peaking at ~415.4 million barrels and holding steady from mid-Feb through March 20.
- **Late March – June 2026:** A **historic release** of ~89.8 million barrels over ~14 weeks, with several weeks drawing 8–10 million barrels. The largest single-week draw (−7.1M) came the week ending April 24. The final reported week (June 26) moderated to −5.5M.

**Driver:** On March 11, 2026, President Trump authorized DOE to release **172 million barrels** — the largest single-country emergency reserve release ever — as part of a coordinated 400-million-barrel IEA release by 32 nations, responding to a Middle East supply crisis (Strait of Hormuz closure / war with Iran).

**Data caveat:** FRED does not carry series WCSSTUS1 (queries failed). Figures were pulled directly from EIA's data navigator and cross-validated against TradingEconomics (325.7M) and YCharts (325.66M).

key_facts: SPR crude oil stocks (EIA series WCSSTUS1) stood at 325,655 thousand barrels (~325.7 million barrels) for the week ending June 26, 2026 — the lowest since May 1983.
This is down ~19.1% year-over-year (from ~402.5 million barrels) and down ~1.67% (−5,536 thousand bbl) from the prior week.
SPR peaked at ~415.4 million barrels in Feb–March 2026 and held flat from mid-Feb through March 20, 2026, after modest fills through late 2025/early 2026.
The historic drawdown began the week of March 27, 2026, accelerating through May and June 2026.
Cumulative draw from the ~415.4M peak (03/20) to 325.7M (06/26) is ~89.8 million barrels over ~14 weeks.
The week ending April 24, 2026 saw a −7.1 million barrel draw, the largest weekly SPR release since the week ending October 7, 2022.
On March 11, 2026, President Trump authorized DOE to release 172 million barrels from the SPR — the largest single-country emergency reserve release in history.
The 172M release was part of a coordinated 400-million-barrel IEA release by 32 member nations in response to a Middle East supply crisis (Strait of Hormuz / war with Iran).
DOE said the release would take ~120 days at planned discharge rates; the first tranche RFP (up to 86 million barrels, structured as an exchange) was issued March 13, 2026.
FRED does not carry series WCSSTUS1; data was obtained directly from EIA and cross-validated by TradingEconomics and YCharts.
SPR authorized storage capacity is 714 million barrels; the June 26 level (~325.7M) is about 46% of capacity.
Weekly draws moderated to −5.5 million barrels for the week of June 26, 2026, down from ~−9 million in prior weeks.
